title: T-115 — UI: AllergenPanel (EU14 presence badges) (NPD-g)
prompt:
# T-115 — AllergenPanel slice

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §17.11.1 + §8.5 (allergen cascade)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/recipe.jsx:103-122 (AllergenPanel)
Prototype-index entry: allergen_cascade (closest catalogued entry; the inline AllergenPanel at recipe.jsx:103-122 is the visual reference for this slice)

## Goal
Single-component slice rendering the EU14 allergen presence panel from the formulation editor. Each of the EU14 allergens shows a presence badge (absent / trace / present) color-coded green/amber/red. When any allergen is detected, an Alert at the bottom warns 'Must be declared on label'. Reactive to formulation state changes.

## Implementation contract
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/allergen-panel.tsx ('use client'). Props: { allergens: AllergenStatus[] } where AllergenStatus = { code: EU14Code, name: string, status: 'absent' | 'trace' | 'present' }.
- Use shadcn Card + Badge + Alert per translation_notes for allergen_cascade.
- Color tokens: absent → muted/outline, trace → amber, present → red. Use accessible Badge variants with text labels (not color alone — aria attributes too).
- EU14 list source: const EU14_ALLERGENS (cereals/gluten, crustaceans, eggs, fish, peanuts, soybeans, milk, nuts, celery, mustard, sesame, sulphites, lupin, molluscs) — declare inside the component file as a constant; production data plumbing happens in T-117 wiring.

## Files (≤ 3)
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/allergen-panel.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/__tests__/allergen-panel.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the panel renders with a mix of absent/trace/present statuses, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/recipe.jsx:103-122 (AllergenPanel), then it displays all 14 EU allergens as badges with status-specific color tokens (present=red, trace=amber, absent=muted) — verified by RTL parity
2. Given at least one allergen has status 'present' or 'trace', when the panel renders, then a shadcn Alert below the badges lists detected allergens and reads 'Must be declared on label' — verified by RTL text + role='alert'
3. Given the parent formulation state changes (new allergens prop), when the panel re-renders, then badge statuses update reactively — verified by RTL rerender test
4. Given the panel renders, then each badge exposes an accessible name via aria-label combining allergen name + status (color is not sole signal) — verified by axe-core/jest-axe

## Test strategy
- RED: allergen-panel.test.tsx asserting 14 badges + status color classes + Alert visibility + reactive rerender + a11y aria-label — before component exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test allergen-panel'

## Risk red lines
- Do not rely on color alone (a11y) — every badge must have aria-label / sr-only text describing status
- Do not own allergen state — controlled via props from parent
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim
